title: Reliable Bash Automation and Tests · System Lens: Script Contract
summary: Turn a fragile command history into a bounded script that validates inputs, preserves data, reports failures, and can be rerun safely. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux script contract increment in a tested idempotent Bash operations script. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.
Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Script Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A report script receives filenames "north 4.csv" and "--dry-run" plus one missing file. Preserve argument boundaries, validate before mutation, clean temporary state, and prove rerunning is idempotent.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Script Contract contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.

content.text: A report script receives filenames "north 4.csv" and "--dry-run" plus one missing file. Preserve argument boundaries, validate before mutation, clean temporary state, and prove rerunning is idempotent.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Script Contract condition decided your prediction.

steps.title: Bound Script Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A report script receives filenames "north 5.csv" and "--dry-run" plus one missing file. Preserve argument boundaries, validate before mutation, clean temporary state, and prove rerunning is idempotent.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Script Contract application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.

content.text: Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A report script receives filenames "north 4.csv" and "--dry-run" plus one missing file. Preserve argument boundaries, validate before mutation, clean temporary state, and prove rerunning is idempotent. A defensible solution applies Script Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a tested idempotent Bash operations script.

content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A script is correct if it succeeds once on the author’s machine. This fails the Script Contract contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Script Contract evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe Linux operations investigation headed "# Evidence: linux-script-contract-1ozb16" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Script Contract into observable performance evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.
